mnemonics: Average Individual Values (AIV) - To find the sample mean, Add all Individual Values and divide by the count.
keyPoints: The sample mean is the arithmetic average of a set of observations.
It is calculated by summing all data points and dividing by the number of points (n).
The sample mean is a primary estimator for the population mean (μ).
commonMistakes: Confusing the formula for a population mean (μ) with a sample mean (x̄).
Forgetting to divide by the sample size (n) after summing the values, resulting in just the total sum.
